title: Developer data-handling training — prod-access engineers lacking training
exclusions: Does NOT cover all engineering staff — focuses on those with production access to regulated data.
evidence: Saved search 'dev_data_handling_training' (weekly) intersects IdP prod-access groups against LMS DPO-approved course completions.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Engineer bob is in the prod-access group for customer-database but has no current DPO course record; the UC fires the next week.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All prod-access engineers have current training; no alert.

dataSources: IdP prod-access group membership, LMS completion records for DPO-approved course ids.
spl:
| rest /services/identity/prod-access-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group scope
| where scope="regulated"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=data_handling_v* earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user group scope last_completion
description: Prevents the 'developers with production access but stale training' anti-pattern that surfaces in GDPR Art.32 enforcement cases.
value: Links IdP posture to training posture — the evidence auditors actually ask for.
implementation: (1) Tag IdP groups for prod access with regulated scope; (2) publish DPO-approved course IDs; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-revoke access after configured overdue window. Indexes required: `index=lms`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=completion`.
visualization: Table of engineers without training, bar chart by team, single-value 'prod-access engineers with current training'. Key fields: `group`, `last_completion`, `scope`, `user`, `user]`.
knownFalsePositives: Freshly-onboarded engineers in the 30-day grace window should be allow-listed by onboarding_date.
grandmaExplanation: We check on prevents the 'developers with production access but stale training' anti-pattern that surfaces in gdpr enforcement cases. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IdP prod-access group membership, LMS completion records for DPO-approved course ids..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag IdP groups for prod access with regulated scope; (2) publish DPO-approved course IDs; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-revoke access after configured overdue window.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/identity/prod-access-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group scope
| where scope="regulated"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=data_handling_v* earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user group scope last_completion
```

Understanding this SPL

**Developer data-handling training — prod-access engineers lacking training** — Links IdP posture to training posture — the evidence auditors actually ask for.

Documented **Data sources**: IdP prod-access group membership, LMS completion records for DPO-approved course ids. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Keeps or drops fields with `fields` to shape columns and size.
• Filters the current rows with `where scope="regulated"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: group, last_completion, scope, user, user].

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of engineers without training, bar chart by team, single-value 'prod-access engineers with current training'.
